title: Scalability Scan
findings: Found one new high-severity scalability issue: v2.2 store-only recall re-walks and re-reads every read-set store multiple times per fab_recall; one review-search O(N) candidate was skipped as an existing issue.

issues_found.title: Store-only recall re-walks and re-reads every read-set store multiple times per request
issues_found.description: walkReadSetStores enumerates all read-set stores via readKnowledgeAcrossStores and readFileSyncs every markdown file; planContext calls computeReadSetRevision and buildCrossStoreRawItems, then fab_recall calls getKnowledgeSections, which calls computeReadSetRevision and buildCrossStoreBodyIndex again. A normal fab_recall therefore pays roughly four full store walks/reads before reading selected bodies, so latency scales linearly with total mounted-store corpus size and is multiplied per request.
issues_found.fix_direction: Introduce a per-request StoreCorpus snapshot containing entries, revision, raw candidates, and id-to-body refs; compute it once in planContext/recall and pass it through to getKnowledgeSections, or memoize by read-set plus directory fingerprint/mtime so unchanged stores are not re-read and re-hashed repeatedly.
